calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Approving Settlement Of Litigation With The State Of California
Off Calendar Nov-22-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Perdue for and with (petitioner) David Keeler.

The Court asked counsel why this was filed in Probate and not in civil. Mr. Perdue stated that they are confused and that they will get it into civil court. The Court stated that this is not an internal trust issue and that if it was for instructions for the trustee to enter into an agreement it would be different. Mr. Perdue stated that there are no other parties involved, that they have notified the beneficiaries and that there are no objections. The Petition for Order Approving Settlement of Litigation with the State of California is off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Yolanda Mazariegos. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order Approving Settlement Of Litigation With The State Of California
Off Calendar Oct-18-2016 Continued To Nov-22-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Approving Settlement Of Litigation With The State Of California
Off Calendar Oct-11-2016 Continued To Oct-18-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Approving Settlement Of Litigation With The State Of California
Off Calendar Sep-27-2016 Continued To Oct-11-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petn. For Order Confirming Certain Assets Are Trust Property
Off Calendar Sep-24-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PCN-93-260769 Conservatorship of Sannas Y. M. Foo and PTR-13-297294 The Sannas Foo 2013 Revocable Living Trust

Appearances: Paul Perdue for and with David Keeler.

Mr. Perdue stated that the Trust does not repeat the provisions of the Will but that it incorporates them by reference and that designation of the Heirs shows consciousness of what Ms. Foo wanted to do. The Petition to Terminate Conservatorship of Estate/Final Account and the Petition for Order Confirming Certain Assets are Trust Property were taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Petn. For Order Confirming Certain Assets Are Trust Property
Off Calendar Aug-06-2014 Continued To Sep-03-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PCN-93-260769 Conservatship of Sannas Foo and PTR-13-297294 The Sannas Foo 2013 Revocable Living Trust.

Appearances: Paul Perdue for and with (conservator) David Keeler.

Mr. Perdue stated that he served the State of California with everything that has been filed, that if the trust in implemented the first order of business is to sell the property and pay the city, and that he will file a declaratory relief action in early spring. Mr. Perdue also provided medical history, explained that there are two obligations for hospitalizations, one in the amount of $300,000.00 and the other in the amount of $1,400,000.00 which is time barred and that there is enough equity to pay the smaller obligation. The court expressed concern about the trust having been created without a substituted judgment and questioned whether Ms. Foo had capacity to execute a trust in May of 2013 and if someone other than Mr. Keeler could file a declaration regarding capacity. Mr. Perdue stated he would get in touch with the attorney who prepared the trust and stated that he could also reach out to the conservatee's health team. The petition to terminate conservatorship of estate & the petition for order confirming trust property were continued to September 3, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Petn. For Order Confirming Certain Assets Are Trust Property
Off Calendar Jul-10-2014 Continued To Aug-06-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PCN-93-260769 Conservatorship of Sannas Y.M. Foo and PTR-13-297294 The Matter of Sannas Foo 2013 Revocable Living Trust

Appearances: Paul Perdue for and with David Keeler (Petitioner/Conservator).

Mr. Perdue addressed the Court's concern with respect to the Decedent's capacity to execute a Trust, the Court's question of whether the matter should proceed by way of probate and the Court's position that it is not willing to approve a Trust without Court supervision. Mr. Perdue also stated that the only thing that is enforceable is the settlement agreement with the City of San Francisco, that the City has a deed of trust, that it's just security on the land and that the City is protected. The Petition to Terminate Conservatorship of Estate was taken under submission. The Court may add it back to calendar for a further declaration regarding capacity.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)
